description: Real, verbatim SUPERAGENT_CAPTURE_DIR capture (2026-07-02, claude-agent-sdk 0.3.197 / CLI 2.1.197) of the premature-idle regression for background SUBAGENTS (task_type 'local_agent'). The agent launches a background Agent ('Sleep 30 seconds', run_in_background:true), gets the 'async_launched' tool_result (carries agentId, NO backgroundTaskId), and ends its turn immediately ('Launched.'). Under this SDK the turn settles right away: result at t=25655 and session_state_changed:'idle' at t=25660 fire while the subagent still has ~28s to run — older SDKs (see background-subagent-completion, 2026-06-11) held result/idle back until background agents finished, which is why no host-side gate ever existed for local_agent. The real completion arrives ~31s AFTER the premature idle: task_updated{patch.status:completed} at t=56832, then session_state_changed:'running' at t=56933, then task_notification{tool_use_id,status:completed} at t=56940, then the wake turn's result (origin task-notification) + the truly-settled idle at t=58642. Correct behavior: the turn-end idle with a background subagent still running must NOT finalize (no session_idle) — it should surface session_waiting_background and keep the session alive until the task's own terminal signal clears it and the final idle settles. Quirk preserved verbatim: the subagent's INNER Bash surfaces in the main stream as unparented task_started{task_type:'local_bash', task_id:'bzlcchicu'} at t=28772 (after the premature idle) + task_notification at t=55775 — these must not re-activate the session or be confused with the local_agent task.
